multi_state_no_waiver.override_summary: No waiver of claims under state franchise law, including fraud in the inducement; no disclaimer of reliance on franchisor statements. Supersedes other terms.

overrides.override_summary: Item 21 amended to include Hyatt Franchising, L.L.C. unaudited balance sheet as of December 31, 2024
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides entity-level financial visibility beyond parent consolidated statements

overrides.override_summary: Standard franchise investment law filing notice plus no-waiver provision
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional disclosure protections for Hawaii franchisees; 7-day advance FDD delivery required

overrides.override_summary: Releases upon renewal/transfer do not apply to Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees in Maryland cannot be forced to release state franchise law claims as condition of renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides potential protection against automatic termination in bankruptcy

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring Maryland Franchise Law claims in Maryland courts (subject to arbitration obligation)
overrides.why_it_matters: Avoids forced travel to Illinois for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: State law governs state franchise claims rather than Illinois law

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Defines statute of limitations for Maryland franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30-day cure period. Void if FA permits termination before expiration without good cause.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significant franchisee protection — franchisor cannot terminate without good cause and must provide cure opportunity

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate franchisee by repurchase or other means for FMV of inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, furnishings upon non-renewal (if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under another mark)
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides economic protection upon non-renewal in certain circumstances

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration/litigation outside Michigan is void. However, franchisor states it will enforce arbitration provision believing provision (f) is unconstitutional.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan prohibits out-of-state dispute resolution, but franchisor explicitly intends to challenge this. Creates legal uncertainty.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer without good cause (which includes transferee qualifications, competitor status, failure to pay/cure)
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits franchisor's discretion to deny transfers

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release/waiver that deprives franchisee of rights under Michigan Franchise Investment Law (may settle claims after entering FA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's statutory rights from being waived by contract

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination with 60 days to cure, except in specified cases (per Minn. Stat. §80C.14)
overrides.why_it_matters: More generous cure periods than standard FA provisions (which have 10-30 day cure periods)

overrides.override_summary: 180 days' notice for non-renewal (per Minn. Stat. §80C.14)
overrides.why_it_matters: Longer notice period gives franchisee more time to plan for non-renewal

overrides.override_summary: Release restrictions per Minn. Rule 2860.4400D — release cannot apply to claims prohibited by law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to pursue state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee cannot waive right to jury trial, forum/remedies, or consent to liquidated damages/termination penalties (per Minn. Rule 2860.4400J). Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: May render liquidated damages provisions unenforceable for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be conducted outside Minnesota (per Minn. Stat. §80C.21). Subject to arbitration obligation.
overrides.why_it_matters: Avoids forced travel to Illinois for dispute resolution

overrides.override_summary: Commissioner has determined termination/liquidated damages to be unfair, unjust, and inequitable. Franchisor will enforce to extent law allows.
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages and termination penalties may be unenforceable for North Dakota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims arising under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's state franchise law claims from being released

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site per state law (unless preempted by FAA). Forum: may bring action in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: May avoid forced arbitration in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to extent required by law
overrides.why_it_matters: State law may supersede Illinois choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Forum restriction void as to claims under Rhode Island Franchise Investment Act § 19-28.1-14
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees can bring Rhode Island FIA claims in Rhode Island courts

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law applies generally, except as otherwise required by law for Rhode Island FIA claims
overrides.why_it_matters: State law governs state franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause (per Section 13.1-564 of Virginia Retail Franchising Act). Grounds that don't constitute 'reasonable cause' may be unenforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Additional termination protection — franchisor must have 'reasonable cause' beyond contractual provisions
